Title: Molecular Characterisation of the Peroxidase Gene Family in Botrytis cinerea and the Role of BcPRD7 in Virulence

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated under H2O2 stress; also upregulated during hyphal development, Arabidopsis thaliana infection, and highly expressed at 4 h during conidial development
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ROS homeostasis and oxidative stress protection; mycotoxins contribute to fungal pathogenicity
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates peroxidase activity, ROS scavenging, growth, stress tolerance, cell integrity, and virulence; affects synthesis and metabolism of mycotoxins
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BcPRD7 is a core peroxidase-family gene highly induced by H2O2 stress, infection, and conidial development. 2. Deletion of BcPRD7 reduced virulence almost completely on Nicotiana benthamiana, impaired infection cushion formation, sporulation, and hyphal morphology. 3. Recombinant BcPRD7 showed peroxidase activity and ΔBcPRD7 had reduced intracellular ROS-related fluorescence and altered transcriptomes enriched for oxidoreductase activity, ion transport, and mycotoxin metabolism. 4. BcPRD7 physically interacts with BcHEX and BcHEX expression increased peroxidase activity, suggesting linkage to Woronin body-related cell integrity.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene knockout; complementation; recombinant protein expression and peroxidase activity assay; ROS assay; RNA-seq; yeast two-hybrid; luciferase complementation; co-immunoprecipitation; virulence assay; stress assays; microscopy
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: H2O2 stress; NaCl; KCl; menadione; Congo Red; fluconazole; host infection
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Deletion causes major defects in growth, sporulation, infection structure formation, stress tolerance, and virulence
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antifungal target for controlling grey mould by disrupting peroxidase-mediated oxidative stress management and Woronin body-associated cell integrity
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper focuses on pathogenicity rather than crop nutrient improvement; metabolically relevant outputs are ROS homeostasis and mycotoxin synthesis/metabolism rather than nutritional quality traits

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated under H2O2 stress; also upregulated during hyphal development, Arabidopsis thaliana infection, and highly expressed at 4 h during conidial development
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ROS homeostasis and oxidative stress response
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates growth, conidiation, infection structure formation, stress tolerance, cell integrity, and virulence
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BcPRD3 is one of four focal peroxidase genes induced under H2O2 stress and during infection/development. 2. Silencing/knockout caused thinner and longer hyphae, reduced sporulation, smaller conidia, delayed and reduced infection structures, and smaller lesions on Nicotiana benthamiana. 3. Mutant showed high sensitivity to oxidative, osmotic, and cell wall/membrane stress and reduced expression of cell-wall-degrading enzyme genes.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene mutant analysis; qPCR; stress assays; virulence assay; microscopy; ROS assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: H2O2 stress; NaCl; KCl; menadione; Congo Red; fluconazole; host infection
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss impairs fungal growth and pathogenicity
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antifungal target for grey mould control
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper indicates ΔBcPRD3 mutant/silenced line among focal genes; direct enzyme assay not reported for BcPRD3

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated under H2O2 stress; also upregulated during hyphal development, Arabidopsis thaliana infection, and highly expressed at 4 h during conidial development
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ROS homeostasis and oxidative stress response
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates growth, conidiation, infection structure formation, stress tolerance, cell integrity, and virulence
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BcPRD8 is a focal peroxidase gene induced under H2O2 stress and infection/developmental stages. 2. Mutation/silencing caused reduced sporulation, altered hyphae and conidia, delayed infection cushion formation, and reduced virulence on Nicotiana benthamiana. 3. Mutant was sensitive to osmotic and oxidative/cell integrity stresses and showed reduced expression of cell-wall-degrading enzyme genes.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene mutant analysis; qPCR; stress assays; virulence assay; microscopy; ROS assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: H2O2 stress; NaCl; KCl; menadione; Congo Red; fluconazole; host infection
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss impairs fungal growth and pathogenicity
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antifungal target for grey mould control
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Direct enzyme activity assay not reported for BcPRD8

Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Upregulated under H2O2 stress; also upregulated during hyphal development, Arabidopsis thaliana infection, and highly expressed at 4 h during conidial development
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ROS homeostasis and oxidative stress response
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Positively regulates growth, conidiation, infection structure formation, stress response, cell integrity, and virulence
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BcPRD10 is one of four focal peroxidase genes with induced expression during stress and infection-related development. 2. Knockout reduced lesion area on Nicotiana benthamiana and impaired fungal development traits. 3. BcPRD10 contributes to stress adaptation, though some stress responses differed from the other focal genes.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Gene knockout; qPCR; stress assays; virulence assay; microscopy; ROS assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: H2O2 stress; NaCl; KCl; menadione; Congo Red; fluconazole; host infection
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Loss impairs fungal development and virulence
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antifungal target for grey mould control
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: BcPRD10 belongs to class I peroxidase subfamily rather than Dyp peroxidase subfamily

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ROS homeostasis linked to peroxidase activity and cell integrity
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes peroxidase activity and likely supports cell integrity and virulence-associated physiology through interaction with BcPRD7
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BcHEX was identified from transcriptomic/functional analyses as encoding the major Woronin body protein. 2. BcHEX physically interacts with BcPRD7 in yeast two-hybrid, luciferase complementation, and co-IP assays. 3. Introducing BcHEX into B05.10 increased peroxidase activity by ~20%, and into ΔBcPRD7 increased activity by ~30% over the mutant level.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y2H; luciferase complementation; co-IP; transgenic expression; peroxidase activity assay
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential antifungal target via disruption of Woronin body integrity and associated peroxidase function
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a peroxidase gene itself; included because it is experimentally validated as a core interactor affecting peroxidase activity in the Results
